per_turn_leaks.student_message: Let's focus on the episodes. Walk me through one from start to finish.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
It starts with a sharp pain in my right side, just below my ribcage. When I breathe in deeply or cough, it feels like someone is squeezing that area really tightly - it's hard to describe, but it's quite uncomfortable and makes me wince.

As for the wheezing, it's more of a high-pitched sound when I exhale, kind of like air rushing out quickly. It doesn't happen all the time, but it does get worse when I'm exerting myself or have been sick recently.

I've also had some discomfort at night, feeling hot and sweaty.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, any changes in your skin — like sweating or flushing?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've been experiencing some discomfort at night that's left me feeling warm and clammy, but I'm not sure if it's related to my current symptoms or just a side effect of something else.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you noticed any changes in your weight or appetite?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've been feeling really off-balance lately and haven't had much of an appetite. Even when I do eat, it doesn't feel like anything's sticking with me for long.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: And your diet — what does a typical day of eating look like?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Honestly, I haven't been paying much attention to my diet lately because everything tastes bland to me and I just don't have an appetite. When I do eat, I usually stick to simple carbs and processed foods - nothing too nutritious or substantial.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Now family history. Any medical problems run in your family?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My mother has asthma, which makes me wonder if there might be a genetic component to my current breathing issues.
